title: Majority Element
description: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element. The majority element is the element that appears more than n / 2 times. You may assume that the majority element always exists in the array.

examples.explanation: In this example, the number 3 appears twice while the other number appears only once. Since 3 appears more than 3 / 2 = 1.5 times, it is the majority element.
Here, the number 2 appears four times and the number 1 appears three times. Since 2 appears more than 7 / 2 = 3.5 times, it is the majority element.
In a single-element array, that element is by definition the majority element because it appears 1 > 1 / 2 times.

hints: Consider using a hash map to count occurrences of each element.
There is an efficient algorithm that runs in linear time and constant space.
Think about properties of majority elements and how they can be exploited.
What happens if you use sorting to identify the majority element?
